etymology.description: 止(止) (zhǐ, "feet") + 人(人) (rén, "person") = a person stops here -> this
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 止(止) (zhǐ, "feet") and 人(人) (rén, "person") to suggest the idea of a person stopping at a particular location, leading to the meaning 'this', indicating something close by or at hand.

examples.chinese: 此时此刻(此時此刻)，我感到非常幸福(我感到非常幸福)。
examples.pinyin: Cǐ shí cǐ kè, wǒ gǎndào fēicháng xìngfú.
examples.english: At this moment, I feel extremely happy.

examples.chinese: 此书是我的最爱(此書是我的最愛)。
examples.pinyin: Cǐ shū shì wǒ de zuì ài.

examples.chinese: 从此以后(從此以後)，我们将每天见面(我們將每天見面)。
examples.pinyin: Cóng cǐ yǐhòu, wǒmen jiāng měitiān jiànmiàn.
examples.english: From this point on, we will meet every day.
